expectations.evidence: Step 1 'Verify SCIM is Enabled' checks that 'Provisioning to App is Enabled' and the provisioning tab is active.
Step 2 checks 'API Token is set' and references Connected Apps & OAuth Tokens in Salesforce Setup. Mentions token validity but frames it as API token rather than OAuth re-authentication flow.

Step 3 covers email, firstName, lastName, username mappings but never mentions FederationIdentifier by name. Uses 'Missing email mapping' as the example issue — this misses the actual most common root cause for SSO-linked provisioning failures.
No mention of Okta Provisioning > Tasks tab for checking error logs. The without_skill guide focuses on Salesforce-side Connected Apps checks rather than Okta's own provisioning task audit trail.
Step 3 'Check User Assignment & Profile Mapping' and 'Then check Profile Mapping' section address the profile assignment requirement.
